title: Fear of Not Doing
html:
The founding axiom in Yahir's founder voice: "Fear of not doing is greater than fear of doing it." First written in the 2019 2019 08 12 keep things simple pitch-and-philosophy scratchpad, alongside the legacy question and the disruption questions — both arrive in the same column, and they've operated as a paired engine ever since.

The same instinct shows up before it's named. The 2018 2018 12 24 normal neutral to everything note title — "Normal > Neutral to Everything" — reads (on the second pass) as a 16-year-old's worry that being neutral, being unweaponized in any single domain, leaves you doing nothing in particular. The 2020 2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist is the inverse proof: 17-year-old Yahir during lockdown juggling stock picks, FBLA admin, dad's loan paperwork, and Walmart applications in parallel — "Opportunity are anywhere and everywhere." That parallelism is the axiom in motion.

The 2021 2021 04 19 circus parable dad-story makes the same point in narrative form: an 18-year-old at the circus undersells himself ("I can imitate birds"), the manager scoffs, and mid-sentence the kid starts flying away. The kid is Yahir; the manager is anyone who'd rather see him sweep floors. Not doing the bird-imitation thing is the failure.

By 2022 (2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream) the axiom has compounded into the voluntary adversity / nonobvious / asking the right question cluster — the same not-doing-fear directing what should get done. By 2024 (2024 11 28 leading thoughts about 6 month adventure) it's the operational rule FITFO (figure it the fuck out): when the fear lands, move. By 2025 (2025 09 23 how im scraping by) it's lived: gym shifts, DoorDash, plasma-donation question marks, Planet Fitness $15/mo — every entry on the bridge-income list is fear-of-not-doing winning over fear-of-doing-the-uncomfortable-thing.

This is the deepest engine in the corpus. Disruption, Rankett, the China gap-year, the Austin move — all of them clear the not-doing bar before they clear any other.

Note: the 2019 voice-memo and the 2019-08-12 keep-things-simple note are both third-party-influenced (the voice memo is a Portland VC speaker; the 08-12 doc is YEBW interview prep). Yahir's own first verifiably-Yahir-voice articulation of fear-of-not-doing is the May 20 2019 risk tolerance line — three months before the YEBW week — which already states "Afraid of the outcome, won't get anything great or beneficial from it."

Source summaries

  • 2019 08 15 voice memo 1852 — Portland VC founder's "someday is a disease" framing (speaker, not Yahir). Companion to 2019 08 12 keep things simple (YEBW interview prep, also third-party-influenced). Yahir's own first verifiably-Yahir articulation of fear-of-not-doing: 2019 05 20 if youre so afraid of failing youll never experience the win (May 2019, three months earlier).
  • 2019 05 20 if youre so afraid of failing youll never experience the win — earliest verifiably-Yahir-voice articulation
  • 2019 08 12 keep things simple — first compressed-axiom phrasing (mixed-provenance: written as YEBW interview prep)
  • 2018 12 24 normal neutral to everything — pre-articulation worry
  • 2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist — parallelism in action
  • 2021 04 19 circus parable — narrative version
  • 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream — voluntary-adversity rebrand
  • 2024 11 28 leading thoughts about 6 month adventure — operational rule
  • 2025 09 23 how im scraping by — fully lived

Connections

  • legacy question — paired engine
  • voluntary adversity — operational sibling
  • nonobvious — what the fear looks for
  • asking the right question — what the fear sharpens
  • fitfo — the action rule
  • bridge income strategies — current real-world expression
  • rankett — the venture it built
